final_answer:
Because I fixed partial plumbing problems instead of replacing the full validator-admission architecture end-to-end.

Why this matters: the code still uses event-shaped `MsgValidatorJoin` (`tx_hash`, `log_index`, `block_number`) in [tx.proto](/srv/repos/gold-chain/bridge/gilt-consensus/proto/giltconsensusv2/stake/tx.proto:29) and handles join in [msg_server.go](/srv/repos/gold-chain/bridge/gilt-consensus/x/stake/keeper/msg_server.go:37), so it is still not BSC-style native validator creation/election logic.

What should be done next:
1. Replace `MsgValidatorJoin` with native validator-creation semantics (operator/consensus/vote key + self-stake rules), not root-chain event fields.
2. Implement BSC-style deterministic candidate selection/election for active validators.
3. Remove validator-add dependence on bridge-style event metadata from the stake admission path.
4. Add strict parity tests for add/update/exit/election behavior, then run full `bridge/gilt-consensus` test suite before calling it complete.
